content:
# Galatians Chapter 1 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/GAL_001_page_001.jpg, GAL_001_page_002.jpg

### Greeting

1. This letter is from Paul, an apostle. I was not appointed by any group of people or any human authority. My commission comes from Jesus Christ himself and from God the Father, who raised Jesus from the dead.

2. All the brothers and sisters here join me in writing this letter to the churches of Galatia.

3. May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.

4. Jesus gave his life for our sins, just as God our Father planned, in order to rescue us from this present evil age.

5. All glory to God forever and ever! Amen.

### There Is Only One Gospel

6. I am shocked that you are turning away so quickly from God, who called you to himself through the grace of Christ. You are following a different way that pretends to be the Good News

7. but is not the Good News at all. You are being fooled by those who deliberately distort the gospel of Christ.

8. Let God's curse fall on anyone, whether it is we ourselves or even an angel from heaven, who preaches a different gospel than the one we preached to you.

9. I say again what we have said before: if anyone preaches any other gospel than the one you already accepted, let that person be cursed.

10. Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God? Am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.

### Paul's Gospel Came Directly from God

11. Dear brothers and sisters, I want you to understand that the gospel I preach is not based on mere human reasoning.

12. I did not receive my message from any human source, and no one taught it to me. Instead, I received it by direct revelation from Jesus Christ.

13. You know what I was like when I followed the Jewish traditions. I violently persecuted God's church. I did my best to destroy it.

14. I was far ahead of my fellow Jews in my zeal for the traditions of my ancestors.

15. But even before I was born, God chose me and called me by his marvelous grace. Then it pleased him

16. to reveal his Son to me so that I would proclaim the Good News about Jesus to the Gentiles. When this happened, I did not rush out to consult with any human being.

17. Nor did I go up to Jerusalem to consult with those who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went away into Arabia, and later I returned to the city of Damascus.

18. Then three years later I went to Jerusalem to get to know Peter, and I stayed with him for fifteen days.

19. The only other apostle I met at that time was James, the Lord's brother.

20. I declare before God that what I am writing to you is not a lie!

21. After that visit I went north into the provinces of Syria and Cilicia.

22. And still the churches of Christ in Judea did not know me personally.

23. All they knew was that people were saying, "The one who used to persecute us is now preaching the very faith he tried to destroy!"

24. And they praised God because of me.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 — "Not appointed by any group of people":** Paul fires his opening salvo before he even says hello. The standard letter opening in the ancient world was "X, to Y, greetings." Paul weaponizes the greeting — he inserts his entire theological argument into the address line. His apostleship is not derived from any human chain of command. It comes directly from the risen Christ. This is not a greeting; it's a thesis statement.

**Verse 6 — "I am shocked":** thaumazo — Paul is genuinely astonished. In most of his letters, he begins with thanksgiving ("I give thanks to God for you..."). Galatians has no thanksgiving section. Paul skips gratitude and goes straight to shock. This is the angriest letter in the New Testament.

**Verses 8-9 — "Let God's curse fall":** anathema — this is the nuclear option of ancient Jewish language. In the Old Testament, things placed under the "ban" (herem) were devoted to total destruction. Paul pronounces this curse twice — once hypothetically (even if we or an angel) and once directly (if anyone). He includes himself in the scope of the curse. The gospel is more important than the apostle.

**Verses 15-16 — Paul's Damascus experience:** Paul never calls it a "conversion" — he describes it as a prophetic call, echoing Jeremiah ("set apart from the womb") and Isaiah ("called from my mother's womb"). He sees himself not as someone who changed religions but as someone whose lifelong calling was finally revealed.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
